2604.14930.title: IE as Cache: Information Extraction Enhanced Agentic Reasoning
2604.14930.paper_summary: Information Extraction aims to distill structured, decision-relevant information from unstructured text, serving as a foundation for downstream understanding and reasoning. However, it is traditionally treated merely as a terminal objective: once extracted, the resulting structure is often consumed in isolation rather than maintained and reused during multi-step inference. Moving beyond this, we propose \textit{IE-as-Cache}, a framework that repurposes IE as a cognitive cache to enhance agentic reasoning. Drawing inspiration from hierarchical computer memory, our approach combines query-driven extraction with cache-aware reasoning to dynamically maintain compact intermediate information and filter noise. Experiments on challenging benchmarks across diverse LLMs demonstrate significant improvements in reasoning accuracy, indicating that IE can be effectively repurposed as a reusable cognitive resource and offering a promising direction for future research on downstream uses of IE.
2604.14930.paper_summary_zh: 資訊擷取旨在從非結構化文本中提取結構化的、與決策相關的信息，作為下游理解和推理的基礎。<lb/>然而，它傳統上僅被視為一個終端目標：一旦提取，所得到的結構往往是孤立消耗，而不是在多步推理過程中保持和重用。<lb/>超越這一點，我們提出了\textit{IE-as-Cache}，一個將資訊擷取重新利用為認知快取的框架，以增強代理推理。<lb/>受到層次計算機記憶的啟發，我們的方法結合了查詢驅動的擷取與快取感知的推理，以動態維護緊湊的中間信息並過濾噪音。<lb/>在各種大型語言模型的挑戰性基準測試中進行的實驗顯示推理準確性有顯著改善，表明資訊擷取可以有效地重新利用作為可重用的認知資源，並為未來關於資訊擷取下游應用的研究提供了一個有前景的方向。
2604.14930.author: Hang Lv et.al.
2604.14930.authors: Hang Lv, Sheng Liang, Hongchao Gu, Wei Guo, Defu Lian, Yong Liu, Hao Wang, Enhong Chen
